audit_limitations: Mac prototype run_s2_v3_full.py would have saved all-gate coefficients to s2_v3_gate_params_per_corpus.csv: line 754 defines gate_param_fieldnames with logistic_a1, erf_a1, algebraic_a1, arctan_a1; line 791 writes the CSV; line 853 populates fields from fits[gate]. The corresponding results/s2_v3_decoupled_five_gate_2026-04-18/ output directory is absent, consistent with an interrupted run.

Completed Windows port run_s2_v3_windows.py had coefficients in memory via decoupled_smooth_model.py, but wrote only BIC/RMSE/k/w diagnostics to s2_v3_per_fit_results.csv: line 117 defines saved fields, line 216 appends reduced-schema rows, and line 408 writes final outputs.

Therefore non-logistic a1,b1,c1,a2,b2,c2 coefficients are unavailable from saved outputs. Recovering them requires either a fresh rerun of the Mac script or a patched Windows script and rerun of the full five-gate sweep.

Logistic coefficients are populated from results/windows_s2_decoupled_v3_results_2026-04-18/outputs/s2_v3_logistic_local_params.csv. Manuscript-cited claims (BIC values, winner counts, spreads, and synthetic recovery) do not depend on non-logistic regime coefficients.
Synthetic-recovery tanh calibration is not interpreted because near-zero RMSE synthetic fits magnify tiny tanh/logistic numerical differences in BIC.
